title-group.article-title: A Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis

abstract.p: This paper defines a stable right-sided discrete quaternion Fourier regime for multichannel spectral analysis. Building on a canonical quaternion lane representation of paired complex channels, it treats each lifted lane as a quaternion-valued sequence x[n] in H and adopts a fixed transform axis i.
The forward transform is X[k] = sum_{n=0}^{N-1} x[n] exp(-i 2πnk/N), with inverse x[n] = (1/N) sum_{k=0}^{N-1} X[k] exp(+i 2πnk/N).

The paper proves exact inversion, right-linearity over the commuting slice subalgebra C_i, spectral power nonnegativity, and a Parseval identity sum_n |x[n]|^2 = (1/N) sum_k |X[k]|^2. These results justify the fixed-axis right-sided transform as a deterministic spectral seam for quaternion-lifted multichannel data.

license.license-p: This is an open-access article distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution License (CC BY 4.0).

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: The first paper in this signal-processing series established a canonical quaternion lift for ordered multichannel RF and sensing captures. The present paper addresses the spectral layer: once a lane has the form x[n] in H, what Fourier convention should be treated as the stable public transform regime?
Because quaternion Fourier analysis admits multiple legitimate conventions, the paper selects one narrow seam: one-dimensional, right-sided, fixed-axis, and lanewise.

sec.title: Scope and signal model
sec.p: Let x = (x[n])_{n=0}^{N-1}, with x[n] in H, be a quaternion-valued lane of finite length N. In the intended application such lanes arise after canonical quaternion lifting of paired complex channels, but the paper studies the spectral transform on one lane first before extending it lanewise to grouped multichannel captures.

sec.title: Algebraic preliminaries
sec.p: The transform kernel lies in the commuting slice C_i = {a + b i : a,b in R} contained in H.
The paper also uses the discrete orthogonality identity for N-th roots of unity inside this commuting slice.

sec.title: Definition of the stable right-sided transform
sec.p: The kernel is placed on the right. Because quaternion multiplication is noncommutative, this is part of the transform definition rather than a cosmetic notational choice.

sec.title: Algebraic properties
sec.p: The paper proves additivity and right C_i-linearity over the commuting slice scalars.

sec.title: Exact inversion
sec.p: The paper proves that F_i^{-1}(F_i{x})[m] = x[m] for all m = 0,...,N-1 by substituting the forward transform into the inverse and collapsing the inner exponential sum with the orthogonality identity.

sec.title: Spectral power and Parseval invariance
sec.p: These results identify spectral power as a real nonnegative invariant and establish the energy identity for the declared normalization.

sec.title: Lanewise multichannel extension
sec.p: For lifted multichannel data Q[g,n], lane spectra are defined by X_g[k] = F_i{Q[g,\cdot]}[k]. The single-lane Parseval identity extends by summing over lanes.

sec.title: Why this convention is stable
sec.p: The fixed-axis right-sided regime is operationally stable because axis choice, kernel placement, inverse convention, and normalization remain fixed across all lanes and uses within the public spectral seam.

sec.title: Scope, limitations, and non-claims
sec.p: The paper does not claim universal superiority over other quaternion Fourier conventions, does not address left-sided, two-sided, higher-dimensional, or axis-adaptive transforms, and does not yet define cross-lane coherence or orientation metrics.

sec.title: Conclusion
sec.p: This paper establishes the spectral layer of the series. The first paper defined the canonical quaternion lift; the present paper defines the fixed-axis right-sided spectral transform on those lifted lanes. The next paper can therefore begin from spectra that are already deterministic, exactly invertible, and energy-controlled.

element-citation.source: Quaternions and Rotation Sequences: A Primer with Applications to Orbits, Aerospace, and Virtual Reality

element-citation.article-title: Singular Value Decomposition of Quaternion Matrices: A New Tool for Vector-Sensor Signal Processing
Hypercomplex Fourier Transforms of Color Images
